passage:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- The microbiome is the community of trillions of microorganisms living in the human digestive tract.
- These microorganisms help break down complex carbohydrates that human enzymes cannot digest.
- Research has linked imbalances in the gut microbiome to conditions such as obesity, depression, and autoimmune disorders.
- A diverse microbiome, supported by a varied diet rich in fiber, is associated with better overall health.

passageMetadata.source: Student research notes on the gut microbiome

stem: The student wants to explain why maintaining a healthy microbiome matters. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The human gut contains trillions of microorganisms that help digest complex carbohydrates human enzymes cannot break down on their own.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A diverse microbiome supported by a fiber-rich diet is linked to better health, while microbiome imbalances have been connected to obesity, depression, and autoimmune disorders.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Research has connected gut microbiome imbalances to several health conditions, including obesity and depression.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Eating a varied diet rich in fiber supports a diverse microbiome, which is the community of microorganisms in the digestive tract.

explanation: Choice B is correct because it presents both the positive outcome of a healthy microbiome (better health from diversity) and the negative consequences of an unhealthy one (links to obesity, depression, autoimmune disorders), making a complete case for why microbiome health matters. Choices C and D each capture only one side of this relationship.

distractorRationale.A: This choice describes what the microbiome does (digestion) but does not explain why maintaining its health matters in terms of broader health outcomes.
distractorRationale.C: This choice mentions the negative consequences of imbalance but omits the positive outcomes of a healthy, diverse microbiome, providing only half the picture.
distractorRationale.D: This choice explains how to support a diverse microbiome but does not articulate why doing so matters in terms of health consequences.
